response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship background, but the resume shows no native Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no demonstrable embedded firmware or LAN-switch experience, no Linux kernel modification work, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switching domain. C/UNIX experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago; primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web platforms. The core competencies for this role are absent.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across multiple domains
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing, refactoring) and teaching/workshop background
Polyglot programmer with some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement is native-level)
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, MACsec, 802.1X, or Linux kernel modification experience
Primary stack is web/TypeScript/DLang, not C for UNIX/embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / switch / security domain experience
No Linux kernel source modification experience
